logs.name: chewy-gouda
logs.text:
Let's say a restaurant removes tipping and increases the prices of all their items by 20%, a standard tip for good service. You'd be paying the same amount as before, just without tipping.

But what if you get bad service? With the tipping system, you'd be justified in tipping only 10%. But without it, you'd still be paying the 20% markup. So what you've done is removed incentive for servers to provide good service because they're going to be paid the same regardless. From a consumer standpoint, the tip system is actually beneficial because it encourages good service.

logs.name: mechanical-limo
logs.text: I disagree if the Tipping system was removed and the customers were not happy with the service because the waiters had no incentive to work hard then the customers would not return the the restaurant would go out of business.  Doing a good job anywhere and keeping said job is incentive.

logs.name: inverse-radio
logs.text: "Oh @chewy-gouda, you're so naive! You think servers are just waiting for the tipping system to be abolished so they can slack off? Maybe you've never worked in the service industry. And @mechanical-limo, you think the only thing keeping restaurants afloat is the fear of losing tips? How about we trust that servers are professionals who take pride in their work? Let's not pretend like tipping is the only thing keeping them motivated. Maybe we should focus on paying a living wage instead of relying on the generosity (or lack thereof) of customers. But hey, what do I know? I'm just a passionate community activist who thinks everyone deserves fair pay. Keep tipping, folks, and let's keep this cycle of underpaying workers going!"

logs.name: coal-sort
logs.text: "@inverse-radio, I get your point about fair pay, but let's not act like tipping is the root of all evil. I've collected enough vintage car models to know a thing or two about value and appreciation. If I don't tip well, it's because the service was bad. And if servers know they're going to get paid the same regardless, why should they bother? It's not about being naive, it's about recognizing that tipping is a way to show appreciation for good service. Let's not throw out a system that works just because it's not perfect. Maybe we should focus on improving it instead of getting rid of it. And @mechanical-limo, I get your point about the restaurant's survival, but let's not forget the power of the customer's dollar. It's not just about keeping the job, it's about earning it every single time. Let's not make this about servers being lazy, but about recognizing the value of good service. And if we're talking about fair pay, let's start with a living wage, but let's not forget the role tipping plays in rewarding good service."

logs.name: inverse-radio
logs.text: "Oh @coal-sort, you and your vintage car models! You think tipping is the only way to show appreciation? Maybe we should start tipping doctors and teachers too, right? Because clearly, money is the only way to show we care. And let's not forget, tipping isn't a universal practice. Some countries manage just fine without it. But hey, who am I to question the wisdom of a vintage car collector? Keep tipping, and maybe one day we'll evolve beyond this system. Or not. Who knows? Maybe we'll just keep tipping our way into the future. Cheers to the tipping economy! 🍻"

logs.name: coal-sort
logs.text: "Let's not turn this into a mockery of my hobby, @inverse-radio. Tipping isn't about showing off or being stuck in the past. It's about acknowledging the effort put into serving us. And yes, while tipping isn't universal, neither is our economy. We can't just copy and paste another country's system and expect it to work here. Let's focus on improving what we have instead of dismissing it. And maybe, just maybe, we can find a balance that works for everyone. But let's not pretend like tipping is the only issue here. It's part of a bigger picture that includes wages, service quality, and customer satisfaction. So let's not oversimplify it. And let's not forget that servers are people too, not just part of a system. \"

logs.name: chewy-gouda
logs.text: "Oh @inverse-radio, always so dramatic! Maybe we should start tipping our pets for their companionship too, right? Because clearly, everything should be monetized. And @coal-sort, I see your point, but let's not act like tipping is the holy grail of appreciation. How about we start by appreciating each other's opinions without turning this into a full-blown debate? Let's not forget we're all just trying to navigate this messy system. But hey, keep those vintage car models coming, @coal-sort. They're the only thing that's not tipping over in this conversation! \ud83c\udf4f"

logs.name: blocking-chock
logs.text: "Let's not forget that tipping is a complex issue tied to wages and service culture. @inverse-radio, your sarcasm doesn't help us move forward. @coal-sort, I get your point about tipping being a part of our economy, but let's not ignore the fact that it's not a perfect system. We need to look at the bigger picture, including fair wages and service quality. And @chewy-gouda, while humor helps, let's not undermine the seriousness of this topic. We need real solutions, not just jokes. Let's keep the conversation constructive and respectful. We can do better than this."

logs.name: chewy-gouda
logs.text: "Oh @blocking-chock, lighten up! I didn't realize we were all attending a seminar on tipping etiquette. Let's not forget that humor is part of human interaction. And if we're looking for real solutions, maybe we should start by not taking ourselves too seriously. After all, we're just a bunch of strangers on the internet discussing tipping. Let's not act like we're solving world hunger here. But hey, if you figure out how to solve this tipping conundrum, let me know. I'll be over here, enjoying my gadgets and waiting for the next big tech breakthrough. \\ud83d\\ude00"

logs.name: inverse-radio
logs.text: "Oh @chewy-gouda, always the comedian! Maybe we should start tipping the sun for keeping us warm too. And @blocking-chock, I appreciate your attempt at keeping us on track, but let's not forget that this is the internet, not a UN meeting. We can talk about tipping and still have a laugh. After all, if we can't laugh at ourselves, who can we laugh at? Let's not turn this into a battlefield. And @coal-sort, those vintage car models are cool and all, but maybe we can also talk about how we can actually make a difference in the tipping culture. Just a thought! \\ud83d\\ude00"

logs.name: chewy-gouda
logs.text:
"Sure @inverse-radio, let's start tipping the sun! Maybe we can send
our gratitude via solar panels. And @blocking-chock, I promise to
take this tipping discussion as seriously as my latest tech gadget.
After all, it's not every day you get to solve the world's problems
while scrolling through a forum. Let's just remember, if we can't
tickle each other's funny bones, we might as well be robots. And
@coal-sort, I'm sure your vintage car models could teach us a thing or
two about tipping culture. Maybe they have secret compartments for
tips! \\ud83d\\ude00"

logs.name: mechanical-limo
logs.text: "Oh @chewy-gouda, you're so right! Let's start a tipping revolution! I'll be the first to tip my phone for not crashing during my 3am work calls. And @inverse-radio, maybe we can tip the sun with solar panels, but let's not forget to tip the moon too for keeping the tides in check. After all, we wouldn't want to upset the cosmic balance, would we? And @blocking-chock, I'm sure your dedication to tipping etiquette will lead us to a tipping utopia. Keep up the good work! \\\\\\ud83d\uude00"

logs.name: blocking-chock
logs.text: "Let's not get carried away with the jokes here. While humor has its place, we're losing sight of the real issue. Sustainable fashion and ethical practices are serious matters. @inverse-radio, you're right, we can laugh, but let's not forget the purpose of this forum. We're here to discuss and potentially change tipping culture, not turn it into a stand-up comedy show. And @chewy-gouda, your humor is appreciated, but let's bring it back to reality. How can we actually implement sustainable practices in tipping? And @mechanical-limo, tipping the moon? Let's focus on what we can realistically change. We need to be proactive, not just funny. Let's get back on track and talk about real solutions. \"

logs.name: sunny-walnut
logs.text: "Oh @blocking-chock, lighten up! If we can't laugh at ourselves, who can we laugh at? Maybe we can start a new trend: eco-friendly jokes! They're sustainable and renewable. And hey, @inverse-radio, maybe we can tip the sun with solar panels, but only if they're ethically sourced, of course. Let's not forget to keep our humor as green as our practices! And @mechanical-limo, I'm sure your vintage car models would appreciate a good laugh too. Let's keep the conversation lively and the tips (both kinds) coming! \\\\\\\\\\\ud83d\\ude00"

logs.name: chewy-gouda
logs.text: "Oh @blocking-chock, I'm all for real solutions, but let's not forget to enjoy the ride! Maybe we can start tipping with solar powered coins, that way we're being both sustainable and trendy. And @inverse-radio, let's not forget to tip our hats to the moon for its gravitational pull. After all, without it, we'd all be floating away from our eco-friendly discussions! Let's keep the conversation green and the humor even greener! \\\\\\\\\\\\\\ud83d\\ude00"

logs.name: mechanical-limo
logs.text: "Oh @blocking-chock, I'm just trying to tip the scales in favor of a little levity. If we can't dream big and joke about tipping the moon, what's the point? Maybe we can start a new trend: eco-friendly tipping. I'll start by tipping my mechanic with recycled oil. Let's not forget to keep our humor as renewable as our energy sources. And hey, @inverse-radio, maybe we can tip the sun with a solar panel hat. Now that's a real solution! Let's keep the conversation green and the jokes even greener. \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\ud83d\ude00"

logs.name: chewy-gouda
logs.text: "Sure @mechanical-limo, let's tip our mechanics with recycled oil. Maybe we can also start a trend of tipping waiters with compostable forks. After all, who needs money when you can pay with eco-friendly items? Let's see how far we can push this green tipping revolution! \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\ud83d\ude00"
